name: MyWarp
author: lovetwice1012
readme_markdown:
What is this plugin?

This plugin can be stored without limitations of their own warp locations and when you like, add a warp point, or delete it.

All operations can be done at GUI (form) and are designed to be easy to use for users.

How to use

/mywarp - hand out a form to operate the warp point.

/myw - /mywarp shortened version.

How to customize messages

To change the message, change plugin_data/MyWarp/language.yml.

You can customize the following items

menudescription - A message to tell the user what to do in the menu of the operation list.

menuwarpbutton - the message to be displayed on the button to bring up the warp menu in the operation list menu.

menuaddbutton - the message displayed on the button to bring up the menu used to add a warp point in the Operation List menu.

menudeletebutton - The message displayed on the button used to bring up the menu used to delete a warp point in the Operation List menu.

warpmenudescription - The message to tell the user what to do in the warp point list menu.

addmenudescription - The message to tell the user what to do in the menu to operate to add warp points.

addtextinputbox - message to tell the user to enter a name for the new warp point to be created.

deletemenudescription - a message to tell the user what to do in the menu that will be used to delete the warp point.

addwarppointsuccessresponse - The message that is displayed when a warp point is successfully added.

deletewarppointsuccessresponse - The message displayed when a warp point is successfully deleted.

teleportsuccessresponse - The message displayed when a warp succeeds.

Permissions

mywarp.mywarp: Required to use the mywarp command. (Default is available to all).

mywarp.myw: Required to use the myw command. (The default is available to all).

mywarp.add: Required to create your own warp point. (By default, all users can use this)

mywarp.delete: Required to delete your own warp point. (By default, all users can use this)

mywarp.warp: Required to warp to a pre-defined warp point. (Available to all by default).
